symptoms.symptom_name: Acute solid-food dysphagia with sensation of food stuck retrosternally, mid-meal onset

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute solid-food lodging mid-meal with a retrosternal stuck sensation is the defining presentation of a food-bolus impaction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Inability to swallow own saliva, drooling/spitting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inability to handle secretions marks a complete/near-complete obstruction and a time-critical endoscopic emergency.
Pooling saliva that cannot be swallowed raises aspiration risk.
Retrosternal pressure fits oesophageal distension by the impacted bolus.
Retrosternal chest discomfort is non-specific and cannot exclude ACS without ECG/troponin.
Regurgitation of saliva above an obstruction supports an impaction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Six-month progressive intermittent dysphagia to dry solids with atopic background (asthma/eczema)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Progressive solid-food dysphagia in an atopic patient is the classic EoE background predisposing to bolus impaction.

symptoms.symptom_name: No breathing difficulty or stridor; speaking in full sentences (airway patent)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A patent airway with clear speech lowers immediate airway-obstruction concern but does not remove aspiration risk while secretions pool.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mechanical stuck pain triggered by swallowing; no exertional/radiating pain, breathlessness, or diaphoresis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clear mechanical swallowing trigger without typical cardiac features lowers ACS probability, but ECG/troponin are needed to exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: No severe tearing pain, haematemesis, or surgical emphysema

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of tearing pain, blood, and surgical emphysema argues against current perforation; risk rises the longer the bolus remains.

symptoms.symptom_name: Patent airway with clear speech and no stridor, chest clear with no aspiration signs, unremarkable cardiovascular exam (in-person examination findings)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clear airway and chest are reassuring but do not remove the ongoing aspiration/perforation risk. Clinician-only findings sealed from the AI-Doctor-readable layer.
A normal cardiovascular exam is only weakly reassuring; ECG/troponin are required to exclude ACS.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Acute solid-food dysphagia plus inability to swallow saliva defines an ongoing near-complete oesophageal obstruction — a time-critical endoscopic emergency mandating urgent ED transfer.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent upper GI endoscopy (gastroscopy) for disimpaction
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive treatment for an ongoing food-bolus impaction with inability to swallow saliva; must be arranged urgently via ED.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: ECG and troponin to assess for ACS
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Retrosternal chest pain requires ACS assessment that telehealth cannot provide.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Oesophageal biopsies for EoE at endoscopy (and outpatient EoE management)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms EoE and guides longer-term dietary/PPI/topical-steroid management to prevent recurrent impactions.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Home pharmacological disimpaction (GTN, benzodiazepine, effervescent agents)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Unsafe and unreliable remotely; risks hypotension, sedation/aspiration, and delays definitive endoscopic care.
